title: Franklin Templeton Files for ETFs That Funnel Stock Dividends Into Bitcoin
summary: Franklin Templeton has filed for two Bitcoin DRIP ETFs that reinvest stock dividends into Bitcoin. This novel structure could make Bitcoin more accessible to traditional investors.
body:
Franklin Templeton, a major asset manager, has filed with the SEC for two new ETFs that would reinvest stock dividends into Bitcoin. Dubbed "Bitcoin DRIP" (Dividend Reinvestment Plan) funds, these ETFs would hold U.S. stocks and automatically convert their dividends into Bitcoin.

The proposed ETFs represent a unique approach to integrating Bitcoin with traditional investment strategies. By reinvesting dividends into Bitcoin, these funds aim to provide investors with exposure to both stocks and cryptocurrency. The exact details of the stocks to be included and the mechanics of the Bitcoin conversion process are yet to be disclosed.

This move could make Bitcoin more appealing to traditional investors who are familiar with dividend reinvestment plans. It lowers the barrier to entry for those who may be hesitant to directly purchase Bitcoin. The ETF structure also provides a regulated and familiar investment vehicle for those new to crypto.

Investors should watch for the SEC's approval of these ETFs, which could set a precedent for similar products. If approved, these funds could pave the way for more innovative investment strategies that bridge traditional finance and cryptocurrency. Potential investors should stay informed about the SEC's decision and the specific details of the ETFs once they are available.
twitterThread: Franklin Templeton wants to turn stock dividends into Bitcoin with new ETFs. A first for traditional investors.
The "Bitcoin DRIP" funds would hold U.S. stocks and automatically convert dividends to Bitcoin. A novel way to blend stocks and crypto.

If approved, these ETFs could make Bitcoin more accessible. Watch for SEC approval and details → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-19-franklin-templeton-proposes-etfs-that-turn-stock-dividends-into-bitcoin
